source: Learning Item-Interaction Embeddings for User Recommendations

concepts.finding: Learns item embeddings from user interaction sequences (not just co-occurrence). Models temporal dynamics of user-item interactions using RNNs. Key insight: interaction sequences capture user intent transitions better than co-occurrence statistics alone.
concepts.implication: Keyword sequences from search sessions capture intent transitions. A keyword following "best budget" is intent-constrained differently than one following "how to fix." Sequence-aware keyword embeddings capture context-dependent intent.
concepts.open_question: How long of a keyword sequence is needed to capture intent? Should the model use unidirectional (left-to-right) or bidirectional context?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Sequence-aware keyword embeddings (from search session sequences) produce more context-relevant intent clusters than bag-of-co-occurrence embeddings.
